source.title: Universal Negro Improvement Association, 1974 CP07265
source.subjec: Buildings--Ohio--Cleveland; Residences--Ohio--Cleveland; Streets--Ohio--Cleveland; East 40th Street (Cleveland, Ohio); Universal Negro Improvement Association; Central neighborhood (Cleveland, Ohio)
source.descri: The photograph is a street level view of the structure at 2200 East 4oth Street. Originally constructed as the residence of Jacob Goldsmith, the building later served as the Jewish Infants Orphan Home, and finally as the local headquarters for the Universal Negro Improvement Association (UNIA). The building was partially destroyed by fire in 1988 and demolished in 1989. At the time this photograph was taken, the building also housed the African Communities League (ACL).
